dictionary_metadata.purpose: Provide a stable, case-specific controlled taxonomy of exactly 20 WS themes derived from the claimant's Witness Statement, for use in mapping Employment Tribunal and Employment Appeal Tribunal judgment signals into the existing WS without duplication, invention, or overreach.
dictionary_metadata.strict_mapping_rule: Every judgment signal must be mapped to exactly one primary theme using theme_priority and global_mapping_rules. theme_id is a stable dictionary identifier and must not be renumbered for priority reasons. theme_priority is the conflict-resolution rank and may differ from the numeric part of theme_id. New themes are not permitted in this dictionary version.
dictionary_metadata.theme_id_rule: theme_id is a stable dictionary address. Its numeric prefix is not a priority rank.
dictionary_metadata.theme_priority_rule: theme_priority is the conflict-resolution rank. Values must be unique integers from 1 to 20 and must exactly match the order in global_mapping_rules.risk_rule.
dictionary_metadata.cross_reference_theme_ids_rule: cross_reference_theme_ids is not pre-populated in this dictionary. It may be populated only by downstream mapping outputs where a signal has secondary relevance to other themes. It must not create duplicate action items.

ws_theme_dictionary.theme_name: Role Evolution and Changed Performance Expectations
ws_theme_dictionary.definition: The documented progression or reclassification of the claimant's role, duties, seniority, or assessment framework, where the performance expectations applied by the employer changed materially over time. This theme concerns the structural role history and the shift in how performance was assessed, not a specific instruction, conditional duty, or later mismatched standard.
include_when: A signal concerns what the claimant's role required during the relevant period.
A signal concerns how the claimant's performance assessment changed over time.
A signal concerns a documented promotion, role reclassification, or title change that materially altered the claimant's performance expectations or assessment framework.
A signal concerns the removal, reduction, or replacement of earlier performance metrics in later evaluations.
A signal relies on or challenges the WS argument that performance expectations changed materially over time.
exclude_when: The signal is about specific management instructions to prioritise projects — map to T02.
The signal is about a duty being conditional rather than continuous — map to T03.
The signal concerns retrospective application of a later role, policy, or standard to earlier conduct — map to T19.
The signal concerns only ticket-resolution output figures — map to T10.
common_subthemes: Earlier evaluations used different metrics from later evaluations.
Later evaluations reframed priorities toward higher-level work, responsibility, or business impact.
Earlier volume or activity metrics were reduced or absent from the later assessment framework.
The claimant's documented role changed before the conduct was later criticised.
The employer's later allegation did not account for the changed role context.
preferred_ws_destination: Background, Role Evolution, Performance Context, and Management Direction
Performance Assessment, Role Evolution, and Management Direction
evidence_anchor_types: Job description, title, or responsibility records
ws_theme_dictionary.duplication_guardrail: T01 is the factual history and structural role-evolution theme. Do not duplicate with T02, which covers specific management instructions; T03, which covers whether an alleged duty was continuous or conditional; or T19, which covers retrospective application of a later or different standard to earlier conduct.
forbidden_uses: Do not create new allegations about role grading or contractual entitlement.
Do not introduce comparative seniority arguments about colleagues.
Do not use this theme to make legal submissions about contract variation.
ws_theme_dictionary.example_mapping_language: Signal: 'The judgment turns on whether the claimant's role or performance expectations had materially changed before the criticised conduct.' → Map to T01.

ws_theme_dictionary.theme_name: Management Direction and Endorsed Work Priorities
ws_theme_dictionary.definition: The specific management direction, approval, endorsement, or allocation of the work, work priority, or working practice later criticised by the employer. This theme concerns what management positively instructed, authorised, encouraged, or accepted, not merely the structural evolution of the role.
include_when: A signal concerns whether the claimant was instructed to perform or prioritise the work later criticised.
A signal concerns management awareness or endorsement of the claimant's work allocation, work priority, or working pattern.

A signal concerns management communications, evaluations, or records endorsing or directing the claimant to perform or prioritise the work or working practices later relied on as the basis for misconduct.
A signal concerns contemporaneous management records or communications showing awareness and endorsement of the claimant's work allocation or working method.
A signal concerns whether the claimant had management authority or direction to prioritise one workstream, task, or responsibility over another.
exclude_when: The signal is about structural role evolution or changed performance expectations — map to T01.
The signal is about the alleged duty being situational or conditional — map to T03.
The signal is about management awareness of a working practice or its effects, followed by continued work without correction — map to T04.
The signal is about absence of corrective instruction — map to T04 or T07 depending on scope.
common_subthemes: Management directed or endorsed the claimant's work priorities.
Performance reviews or management communications encouraged the work later criticised.
Internal records show management allocation of the claimant toward particular work.
The criticised work was management-directed rather than self-selected side activity.
Management knew and accepted the claimant's work allocation or working method.
preferred_ws_destination: Management direction and endorsed work priorities
Performance evaluations and management communications
evidence_anchor_types: Status updates or communications showing directed work
ws_theme_dictionary.duplication_guardrail: T02 is the affirmative instruction theme: what management told or encouraged the claimant to do. T03 is conditionality of the alleged duty. T07 is absence of warning. Do not map all role-related material to T02 merely because management is mentioned.
forbidden_uses: Do not assert management negligence or bad faith unless the WS expressly supports it.
Do not introduce implied contractual terms.
Do not characterise management motives beyond what the WS states.
ws_theme_dictionary.example_mapping_language: Signal: 'Management directed or endorsed the work priority or working practice later relied on as misconduct.' → Map to T02.

ws_theme_dictionary.theme_name: Conditional or Situational Work Obligation
ws_theme_dictionary.definition: The claimant's case that the obligation relied on by the employer was conditional, situational, secondary, or triggered only in defined circumstances rather than a continuous primary duty. This includes queue coverage during the senior role, but also covers any comparable mismatch between the alleged duty and how that duty actually operated in practice.
include_when: A signal concerns whether the claimant was required to perform the alleged duty continuously or only in defined circumstances.
A signal concerns when the disputed obligation was expected to arise in practice.
A signal refers to queue work, support work, coverage, escalation, or availability being needed only sometimes or during demand-triggered periods.
A signal concerns whether the alleged duty was primary, secondary, conditional, or subordinate to other assigned work.
A signal concerns the claimant's reasonable understanding of practical work expectations before a later disciplinary standard was applied.
exclude_when: The signal concerns positive project-priority instructions — map to T02.
The signal concerns quantitative event timing against duty periods or schedules — map to T08.
The signal concerns retrospective application of a later role, policy, or standard to earlier conduct — map to T19.
common_subthemes: Duty or coverage described as occasional, demand-led, escalation-led, or situational.
Queue participation linked to escalation or client-demand periods.
Alleged duty not treated as the claimant's primary output in practice.
No instruction that the alleged duty had to override project, testing, escalation, or other assigned work.
Claimant's belief that competing assigned work did not require separate permission to step away from the alleged duty.
preferred_ws_destination: Role definition and practical duty expectations
Appeal hearing explanation of conditional coverage model
evidence_anchor_types: Role documents, rota records, allocation records, or procedure documents
Oral communications concerning conditional or escalation-only obligation
Absence of queue-volume criticism in later evaluations
Project-status email with no queue focus
ws_theme_dictionary.duplication_guardrail: T03 answers whether the alleged duty was continuous or conditional in practice. T02 answers what management positively instructed or endorsed. T19 answers whether a later or different role standard was applied retrospectively. Do not merge these themes.
forbidden_uses: Do not assert that the claimant had no relevant obligation at all.
Do not introduce contractual arguments about the disputed duty.
Do not speculate about SLA thresholds or operational coverage requirements beyond the WS.
ws_theme_dictionary.example_mapping_language: Signal: 'The case turns on whether the alleged duty was a continuous obligation or only arose in particular circumstances.' → Map to T03.

ws_theme_dictionary.theme_name: Management Awareness and Continued Working Without Correction
ws_theme_dictionary.definition: Management awareness, actual or put on notice, of the claimant's working practice or its effects, followed by continued permission, tolerance, silence, or lack of corrective instruction before the same conduct was later treated as misconduct. This includes the July 2023 system-effects discussion as a specific evidence anchor, but is not limited to that event.
include_when: A signal concerns whether management knew or was put on notice of the claimant's working practice or its operational effects.
A signal concerns the claimant explaining the working practice or system effects to a manager or Team Leader.
A signal concerns whether management allowed the claimant to continue working in the same way after an opportunity to correct, stop, restrict, or reprioritise the practice.
A signal concerns whether earlier management tolerance, silence, or acquiescence undermines a later misconduct allegation.
A signal concerns whether a discussion with management amounted to a warning, corrective instruction, or clear stop instruction.
A signal concerns the July 2023 discussion as a specific example of management awareness and no instruction to stop.
exclude_when: The signal is about technical causation generally — map to T05.
The signal is about a broader absence of warning without a specific management-awareness or opportunity-to-correct event — map to T07.
The signal is about positive project-priority instructions or affirmative management direction — map to T02.
The signal concerns first disciplinary framing on 19 March 2024 — map to T06 or T07 depending on issue.
common_subthemes: Manager or Team Leader knew, observed, or was put on notice of the working practice or its effects.
Claimant explained the reason for the working practice.
Management did not instruct the claimant to stop, restrict, reprioritise, or change the practice.
Claimant continued the same working practice openly after management awareness.
Subsequent performance evaluation or management conduct reinforced continued work rather than correcting behaviour.
Later misconduct framing is inconsistent with earlier management tolerance, silence, or acquiescence.
Respondent's later oral recollection not supported by contemporaneous written record.
preferred_ws_destination: Management awareness and continued working without correction
Reasonable reliance after management awareness
July 2023 identification of system effects without instruction
evidence_anchor_types: Hearing minutes recording no instruction to stop
Absence of written corrective record after management awareness
Emails, DSAR records, or performance records showing continued work
Claimant's account of management awareness and no corrective instruction
ws_theme_dictionary.duplication_guardrail: T04 requires management awareness or an opportunity to correct, followed by continued work without a clear corrective instruction. Use T07 for general no-warning patterns without a specific awareness/acquiescence event; T02 for positive management instruction; and T05 for technical causation.
forbidden_uses: Do not allege bad faith by the Team Leader unless supported by the WS.
Do not allege falsification of records.
Do not infer management awareness unless the WS or evidence anchor supports actual knowledge, notice, observation, or an opportunity to correct.
Do not use this theme to invent technical detail beyond the WS.
ws_theme_dictionary.example_mapping_language: Signal: 'The manager knew how the claimant was working, did not tell him to stop, and allowed the same practice to continue before later treating it as misconduct.' → Map to T04.

ws_theme_dictionary.theme_name: Technical Causation Versus Disciplinary Inference
ws_theme_dictionary.definition: The distinction between a technical or operational event arising from the claimant's legitimate work practice and the disciplinary inference that the event was deliberately created to avoid obligations or duties. This theme concerns the bridge from technical occurrence to culpable intent.
include_when: A signal concerns the cause of a technical or operational event arising from the claimant's work practice.
A signal concerns whether a technical occurrence proves intentional misconduct or deliberate avoidance of duties.
A signal concerns whether a technical event can support a disciplinary inference of culpable intent.
A signal concerns the employer's inference from system or operational data to deliberate misconduct.
A signal concerns the distinction between awareness of a technical effect and awareness of its disciplinary significance.
exclude_when: The signal is about management awareness of the practice or effects and absence of correction after that awareness — map to T04.
The signal is about event timing against duty periods or schedules — map to T08.
The signal is about missing logs — map to T09.
The signal is about positive ticket or attendance evidence — map to T10.
common_subthemes: The claimant's work practice required operating in a way that caused a technical side-effect.
The technical event was a consequence of legitimate assigned work, not deliberate avoidance.
Awareness that a work practice could cause technical effects is not the same as awareness of disciplinary significance.
The occurrence of a technical event does not itself prove missed obligations or deliberate avoidance.
An inference of deliberate misconduct requires a causal mechanism and supporting evidence, not raw event counts alone.
preferred_ws_destination: Technical causation versus avoidance inference
Environment switching as routine aspect of project delivery
First presentation of allegations and lack of prior notice
ws_theme_dictionary.duplication_guardrail: T05 is the inference theme. Do not map raw-data or methodology problems here if T08 or T09 fits more precisely. T05 is for the bridge from technical event to alleged misconduct.
forbidden_uses: Do not introduce unsupported technical detail about Bloomberg systems.
Do not allege fabricated crash data unless the WS supports it.
Do not claim technical causation conclusively disproves intent unless supported by evidence.
ws_theme_dictionary.example_mapping_language: Signal: 'The employer inferred deliberate misconduct from a technical system event caused by the claimant's work practice.' → Map to T05.

ws_theme_dictionary.theme_name: Early, Consistent, and Documented Defence Chronology
ws_theme_dictionary.definition: The claimant's case that the core explanation or defence was raised at the first reasonable opportunity and maintained consistently through the investigation, disciplinary hearing, appeal, and WS. This theme covers both positive consistency evidence and adversarial changed-account allegations requiring rebuttal mapping.
include_when: A signal concerns timing or consistency of the claimant's explanation.
A signal concerns whether an explanation was raised early or late.
A signal concerns whether the claimant changed account between investigation, disciplinary hearing, appeal, and WS.
A signal concerns written or oral records showing the claimant's explanation was advanced before or during the disciplinary process.
A signal concerns whether later recollections, minutes, or outcome letters fairly characterise the chronology of the claimant's explanation.
exclude_when: The signal is only about the content of the technical explanation — map to T05.
The signal is about hearing conditions preventing full presentation — map to T12.
The signal is about minutes inaccurately recording the explanation — map to T13.
common_subthemes: Core explanation raised at the first reasonable opportunity.
Same substantive explanation maintained across meetings, hearing, appeal, and WS.
Written defence or contemporaneous records support the chronology.
Adverse changed-account allegation requires rebuttal against the recorded chronology.
Distinction maintained between factual awareness and disciplinary culpability.
preferred_ws_destination: First presentation of allegations and early explanation
Submission of written defence
Appeal hearing account
evidence_anchor_types: Appeal grounds and appeal hearing notes
ws_theme_dictionary.duplication_guardrail: T06 takes priority over all other themes where the signal is about timing, consistency, or changed explanation. It is used both for positive consistency signals and for adversarial inconsistency attacks that need rebuttal mapping. Do not split the investigation, written response, disciplinary hearing, appeal, and WS chronology into separate themes.
forbidden_uses: Do not overstate what was said at early meetings beyond the records.
Do not invent detail to fill gaps in meeting notes.
Do not assume the tribunal will prefer the WS over contemporaneous minutes without addressing conflicts.
ws_theme_dictionary.example_mapping_language: Signal: 'The employer argues the claimant changed his explanation.' → Map to T06 as a consistency-risk/rebuttal signal. Do not add the adverse proposition as a positive WS fact; use T06 to test and reinforce the existing chronology against that attack.

ws_theme_dictionary.theme_name: Absence of Prior Warning, Corrective Instruction, or Guidance
ws_theme_dictionary.definition: The broader pattern that no warning, performance improvement plan, corrective instruction, or formal or informal guidance was issued in relation to the conduct or working practices later relied upon as the basis for disciplinary action, before the disciplinary process was initiated.
include_when: A signal concerns absence of prior warning or corrective instruction.
A signal concerns whether the claimant knew conduct could be disciplinary before March 2024.
A signal concerns whether management used available performance mechanisms to identify concern.
A signal concerns positive evaluations despite the later misconduct allegation.
A signal concerns reasonable reliance on silence or absence of correction over time.
exclude_when: The signal concerns management awareness of a working practice or its effects, followed by continued work without correction — map to T04.
The signal concerns short notice of the disciplinary hearing — map to T11.
The signal concerns predetermination — map to T15.
common_subthemes: No formal warning.
No informal corrective warning.
No performance improvement plan.
No recorded instruction to stop or change working practices.
Positive performance evaluations instead of corrective guidance.
Absence of contemporaneous concern despite management awareness.
preferred_ws_destination: Reasonable reliance after management awareness
Notice and disciplinary-prejudice sections
Appeal hearing explanation
evidence_anchor_types: Hearing minutes confirming no stop instruction
ws_theme_dictionary.duplication_guardrail: T07 is longitudinal no-warning evidence. T04 is management awareness/acquiescence followed by continued work without correction. Keep both distinct.
forbidden_uses: Do not state as a legal proposition that a warning is always required before dismissal for gross misconduct.
Do not speculate about undisclosed HR records.
Do not introduce comparator warning evidence unless mapped to T17.
ws_theme_dictionary.example_mapping_language: Signal: 'The claimant had no prior warning that the conduct was unacceptable.' → Map to T07.

ws_theme_dictionary.theme_name: Failure to Align Event Data with Relevant Duty Periods
ws_theme_dictionary.definition: The methodological flaw that the respondent relied on undifferentiated event counts, system data, or incident records without separating events occurring during periods when the relevant duty actually applied from events outside those duty periods. This includes queue-time versus non-queue-time crash analysis, but is not limited to crash data.
include_when: A signal concerns correlation between event timestamps and the periods when the relevant duty applied.
A signal concerns whether alleged events occurred during periods when the claimant was expected to perform the disputed duty.
A signal concerns weekend, out-of-hours, leave, non-duty, project-only, or otherwise irrelevant entries being included in an adverse count.
A signal concerns the assumption that every recorded event was linked to the disputed duty without testing that timing assumption.
A signal concerns methodological defects in the time-period segmentation or duty-period analysis.
exclude_when: The signal concerns technical cause of crashes — map to T05.
The signal concerns missing raw logs — map to T09.
The signal concerns positive output evidence — map to T10.
The signal concerns whether the underlying duty was conditional rather than continuous — map to T03.
The signal concerns overall investigative reasoning rather than the specific data-to-duty-period alignment flaw — map to T14.
common_subthemes: Event figures not correlated with duty schedules.
No distinction between relevant duty-time and non-duty-time events.
Weekend and out-of-hours entries included in the sample.
Status labels or availability indicators treated as equivalent to active duty without further analysis.
Assumption-based conclusion rather than timestamp and duty-period verification.
preferred_ws_destination: Failure to distinguish relevant duty periods from irrelevant event data
Failure to evidence allegations and selective treatment of objective evidence
evidence_anchor_types: Queue allocation, rota, schedule, attendance, or duty-period records if available
DSAR material showing weekend, out-of-hours, or non-duty entries
ws_theme_dictionary.duplication_guardrail: T08 is the data-to-duty-period alignment theme. Do not use T14 if this more specific methodology flaw applies. Use T03 where the issue is the practical scope of the duty itself; use T09 where the underlying data is missing.
forbidden_uses: Do not invent statistical analysis.
Do not assert specific crash counts not in the WS.
Do not speculate what a corrected analysis would prove.
ws_theme_dictionary.example_mapping_language: Signal: 'The employer did not test whether the adverse events occurred during periods when the claimant was actually expected to perform the disputed duty.' → Map to T08.

ws_theme_dictionary.theme_name: Missing Raw System Logs and Evidence Deficiency
ws_theme_dictionary.definition: The evidential gap arising from the absence of raw system logs or underlying records showing incident timing, operational status, task impact, and work-activity consequence. The respondent relied on high-level figures rather than verifiable underlying data, despite the claimant's request for logs.
include_when: A signal concerns missing logs or missing underlying data.
A signal concerns reliance on summary figures rather than raw records.
A signal concerns inability to verify or test crash figures.
A signal concerns DSAR requests for system logs.
A signal concerns absence of records showing client or operational impact.
exclude_when: The signal concerns failure to align event timing with relevant duty periods — map to T08.
The signal concerns documents not provided before the disciplinary hearing — map to T11.
The signal concerns comparator data — map to T17.
common_subthemes: Formal request for underlying system logs.
No raw logs provided.
High-level summary figures relied upon.
No logs showing exact crash timing and queue status.
No data showing client impact or missed tickets.
preferred_ws_destination: DSAR request and absence of raw system logs
Failure to evidence allegations
evidence_anchor_types: Disclosure record showing absence of logs
ws_theme_dictionary.duplication_guardrail: T09 is about missing evidence. T08 is about flawed segmentation. T14 is about overall investigative reasoning. Use the most specific theme first.
forbidden_uses: Do not speculate what missing logs would have shown.
Do not allege destruction or suppression of evidence unless the WS supports it.
Do not turn disclosure issues into legal submissions inside the dictionary.
ws_theme_dictionary.example_mapping_language: Signal: 'The employer relied on summary data without producing raw logs.' → Map to T09.

ws_theme_dictionary.theme_name: Objective Counter-Evidence to Misconduct Narrative
ws_theme_dictionary.definition: Objective work-output records, attendance records, performance evaluations, and client or colleague feedback that are logically inconsistent with the deliberate avoidance or misconduct allegation. This theme consolidates all categories of objective counter-evidence rather than treating each record type as a separate theme.
include_when: A signal concerns objective evidence inconsistent with the alleged avoidance of work or obligations.
A signal concerns the claimant's work-output or task-completion records during the relevant period.
A signal concerns attendance records, including out-of-hours or voluntary attendance, inconsistent with the misconduct allegation.
A signal concerns positive performance evaluations or client feedback that contradict the misconduct inference.
A signal concerns continued ownership or completion of assigned work during the periods the employer relies on as evidence of avoidance.
exclude_when: The signal concerns role expectations — map to T01, T02, or T03.
The signal concerns missing logs — map to T09.
The signal concerns comparator treatment of another person — map to T17.
common_subthemes: Work-output records compare favourably with the alleged underperformance or avoidance narrative.
Respondent's own evidence shows claimant ownership, completion, attendance, or continued work activity.
Attendance or access records are inconsistent with the misconduct narrative.
Positive performance evaluations.
Client, colleague, or stakeholder feedback contradicts the misconduct inference.
Logical inconsistency between avoidance narrative and continued output.
preferred_ws_destination: Objective work-output evidence and inconsistency of misconduct allegation
Attendance or access evidence and failure to engage with it
Objective evidence countering intent
ws_theme_dictionary.duplication_guardrail: T10 consolidates objective output, attendance, access, performance, and feedback evidence. Do not split each evidence type into a separate theme. Use T17 for comparator-specific evidence.
forbidden_uses: Do not introduce unverified statistics.
Do not claim the evidence conclusively disproves intent unless that is supported.
Do not merge this with comparator arguments.
ws_theme_dictionary.example_mapping_language: Signal: 'The claimant's objective output undermined the alleged avoidance inference.' → Map to T10.

ws_theme_dictionary.theme_name: Short Notice and Pre-Hearing Procedural Prejudice
ws_theme_dictionary.definition: The claimant's case that very short notice of the disciplinary hearing and lack of advance documents caused practical prejudice by limiting preparation, evidence gathering, document review, accompaniment, and ability to respond to serious allegations.
include_when: A signal concerns notice of the disciplinary hearing.
A signal concerns ability to prepare before the hearing.
A signal concerns failure to provide documents in advance.
A signal concerns inability to arrange accompaniment or advice.
A signal concerns respondent procedure requiring notice and documents.
exclude_when: The signal concerns hostile conduct during the hearing — map to T12.
The signal concerns failure to adjourn after issues arose — map to T16.
The signal concerns missing system logs generally — map to T09.
common_subthemes: Two days' notice of hearing.
Documents not provided in advance.
Weekly logs requested but not provided.
Limited time to prepare or seek accompaniment.
Respondent's procedure requiring sufficient information and documents.
preferred_ws_destination: Notice of disciplinary hearing and procedural prejudice
Failure to comply with respondent's own disciplinary procedure
ws_theme_dictionary.duplication_guardrail: T11 is pre-hearing prejudice. T16 is during-hearing failure to adjourn. T12 is hostile conduct. Keep these separate.
forbidden_uses: Do not make standalone legal submissions about procedural law.
Do not speculate beyond the WS about what extra preparation would have achieved.
Do not merge short notice with all procedural complaints.
ws_theme_dictionary.example_mapping_language: Signal: 'The claimant was unable to prepare because of short disciplinary notice.' → Map to T11.

ws_theme_dictionary.theme_name: Hearing Conduct Affecting Ability to Present Defence
ws_theme_dictionary.definition: The claimant says the disciplinary hearing was affected by hostile, dismissive, interruptive, or otherwise unfair conduct that impaired the ability to present the defence calmly and coherently. Linguistic disadvantage or communication difficulty may be relevant where it explains or corroborates the practical prejudice.
include_when: A signal concerns conduct of participants during the disciplinary hearing.
A signal concerns interruptions preventing full explanation.
A signal concerns dismissive or derisive remarks directed at the claimant's manner of expression during the hearing.
A signal concerns the claimant's ability to present the case under hearing conditions.
A signal concerns linguistic disadvantage, communication difficulty, or a later translator request as consequence or corroboration.
exclude_when: The signal concerns inaccurate minutes — map to T13.
The signal concerns predetermination or closed mind — map to T15.
The signal concerns short notice before the hearing — map to T11.
common_subthemes: Interruption of claimant's explanation.
Dismissive or derisive remarks during the hearing.
Communication difficulty or linguistic disadvantage affecting presentation.
Destabilising effect as stated by claimant.
Later translator request as corroborative context where relevant.
preferred_ws_destination: Disciplinary hearing conduct and ability to present defence
evidence_anchor_types: Hearing minutes if they record relevant exchanges
ws_theme_dictionary.duplication_guardrail: T12 is about hearing conduct and ability to present the defence. T13 is about the written record. T15 is about predetermined outcome. Do not merge.
forbidden_uses: Do not convert this into a discrimination claim unless separately pleaded and evidenced.
Do not attribute improper motive to any individual beyond what the WS supports.
Do not assert objective impairment as tribunal fact; frame as claimant's account unless independently evidenced.
ws_theme_dictionary.example_mapping_language: Signal: 'The claimant says hostile interruptions impaired the ability to present the defence.' → Map to T12.

ws_theme_dictionary.theme_name: Inaccurate or Selective Hearing Minutes
ws_theme_dictionary.definition: The claimant's case that the official disciplinary hearing minutes did not fairly or fully record the claimant's explanations, denials, and key points, while recording adverse questions or assertions more prominently.
include_when: A signal concerns accuracy of hearing minutes.
A signal concerns omitted or diluted claimant explanations.
A signal concerns whether minutes captured denial of intentional misconduct.
A signal concerns the recorded exchange that no instruction to stop was given.
A signal concerns conflict between minutes and claimant's account.
exclude_when: The signal concerns conduct during hearing — map to T12.
The signal concerns predetermination — map to T15.
The signal concerns appeal failure to address inaccuracies — map to T18.
common_subthemes: Minutes record adverse questions but omit or dilute answers.
Denial of intent not fairly reflected.
No instruction to stop recorded in claimant's favour.
Pre-hearing written defence supports what claimant intended to convey.
Official minutes create credibility contest if not anchored.
preferred_ws_destination: Official minutes and their accuracy
ws_theme_dictionary.duplication_guardrail: T13 is the record-accuracy theme only. Do not use it for general hearing unfairness unless the minutes are specifically at issue.
forbidden_uses: Do not allege deliberate falsification of minutes unless supported by the WS.
Do not reconstruct the hearing beyond the claimant's evidence.
Do not rely solely on assertion where contemporaneous support exists elsewhere.
ws_theme_dictionary.example_mapping_language: Signal: 'The minutes omitted the claimant's key explanation.' → Map to T13.

ws_theme_dictionary.theme_name: Overall Failure to Verify Evidential Assumptions
ws_theme_dictionary.definition: The residual decision-making failure where the respondent drew disciplinary conclusions from material assumptions without verifying the factual predicates necessary to support them. This theme is used only when the signal concerns the overall reasonableness or balance of the evidential assessment and no more specific evidential, procedural, comparator, appeal, predetermination, or management-awareness theme applies.
include_when: A signal concerns overall adequacy of the evidential investigation after checking that no more specific theme captures the defect.

A signal concerns decision-maker reliance on material assumptions rather than verified facts where the assumption is not specifically about event timing, missing records, counter-evidence, comparator treatment, or appeal failure.
A signal concerns failure to weigh exculpatory evidence as part of the overall decision where the evidence does not fit T08, T09, T10, T11, T16, T17, or T18 more precisely.
A signal concerns whether the employer conducted a balanced evidential assessment across the case as a whole.
A signal concerns gross misconduct being found without verifying necessary factual predicates and the verification failure is not already captured by a more specific theme.
exclude_when: The signal concerns management awareness and continued work without correction — map to T04.
The signal concerns event-data alignment with relevant duty periods specifically — map to T08.
The signal concerns missing logs specifically — map to T09.
The signal concerns ticket, badge, or performance counter-evidence specifically — map to T10.
The signal concerns pre-hearing disclosure or notice — map to T11.
The signal concerns predetermination or closed mind — map to T15.
The signal concerns failure to adjourn — map to T16.
The signal concerns comparator or differential-treatment evidence — map to T17.
The signal concerns appeal-stage failure to cure defects — map to T18.
common_subthemes: Material factual premise accepted without adequate verification.
No verification of missed tickets or client impact.
Failure to incorporate counter-evidence into decision.
Selected system metrics relied on without balanced assessment.
Gross misconduct conclusion drawn before evidential assumptions were tested.
preferred_ws_destination: Failure to evidence allegations and selective treatment of objective evidence
Failure to comply with procedure
evidence_anchor_types: Badge and ticket evidence not engaged with
Decision letter or appeal outcome reasoning
ws_theme_dictionary.duplication_guardrail: Do not map to T14 where a more specific theme applies. T14 is a residual overall-evidence theme, not a default home for every investigation criticism.
forbidden_uses: Do not use T14 as a catch-all for every evidence problem.
Do not speculate what a proper investigation would have found.
Do not turn this into legal submissions inside the dictionary.
ws_theme_dictionary.example_mapping_language: Signal: 'The employer's decision rested on untested assumptions overall.' → Map to T14 only if T04, T08, T09, T10, T11, T15, T16, T17, or T18 is not more specific.

ws_theme_dictionary.theme_name: Predetermination, Closed Mind, or Pre-Decided Outcome
ws_theme_dictionary.definition: The claimant's case that the disciplinary process, dismissal decision, or appeal period showed a closed mind or pre-decided outcome before genuine consideration of the claimant's defence. This includes internal scripts, pre-drafted outcomes, prior decision meetings, communications treating dismissal reasons as already established, or appeal-period communications inconsistent with an open-minded process.
include_when: A signal concerns whether the disciplinary outcome was decided before the hearing.
A signal concerns a scripted pre-hearing document or pre-drafted termination outcome.
A signal concerns a prior meeting, internal discussion, or management communication showing the decision-maker's mind was already made up.
A signal concerns internal communication of dismissal reasons while appeal was pending.
A signal concerns LinkedIn or WhatsApp evidence of during-appeal disclosure.
A signal concerns open-mindedness or independence of the process based on documentary, timeline, or communication evidence.
exclude_when: The signal concerns substantive failures in the appeal outcome — map to T18.
The signal concerns comparator evidence ignored on appeal — map to T17.
The signal concerns Fishbowl or anonymous forum material — map to T20 only.
The signal concerns hostile conduct during hearing — map to T12.
common_subthemes: Pre-hearing script anticipated denial.
Pre-drafted termination outcome.
Decision-maker or panel had reached a settled view before hearing the defence.
Internal meeting or communication treated dismissal as already decided.
Hearing mirrored scripted structure.
LinkedIn contact while appeal pending.
WhatsApp exchange stating team had been told claimant manipulated system.
Dismissal allegation communicated as established conclusion before appeal completion.
preferred_ws_destination: Predetermination evidence in the hearing
Predetermination evidence during pending appeal
ws_theme_dictionary.duplication_guardrail: T15 requires evidence of closed mind, pre-decision, scripted outcome, or appeal-period disclosure inconsistent with an open process. Use T18 for appeal reasoning failures without pre-decision evidence. Fishbowl material must not be mapped here; it belongs exclusively to T20 unless independently evidenced elsewhere in the WS.
forbidden_uses: Do not use Fishbowl as predetermination evidence.
Do not speculate about management motives beyond the documents.
Do not overstate the WhatsApp evidence unless date, sender, source, and content are clear.
ws_theme_dictionary.example_mapping_language: Signal: 'The outcome appeared predetermined because internal documents or communications show the decision was effectively made before the claimant's defence was considered.' → Map to T15.

ws_theme_dictionary.theme_name: Failure to Adjourn or Investigate New Points Raised at Hearing
ws_theme_dictionary.definition: The claimant's case that, once material evidential or factual points were raised during the disciplinary hearing, a reasonable process required adjournment or further investigation, including review of relevant records, re-engagement with relevant witnesses, and consideration of contemporaneous evidence. The hearing was not adjourned despite the respondent's procedure or fairness requiring further inquiry.
include_when: A signal concerns failure to adjourn during or after the disciplinary hearing.
A signal concerns failure to investigate new points raised at the hearing.
A signal concerns failure to re-engage relevant witnesses after disputed instructions or facts were raised.
A signal concerns failure to review contemporaneous records before reaching outcome.
A signal concerns the respondent's own adjournment procedure.
exclude_when: The signal concerns short notice before the hearing — map to T11.
The signal concerns appeal-stage failure to investigate — map to T18.
The signal concerns overall evidence verification rather than adjournment as a procedural safeguard — map to T14.
common_subthemes: New points raised regarding event timing and duty-period correlation.
Disputed instruction or factual issue not tested against documents or witnesses.
No adjournment despite material evidential gaps.
No post-hearing investigation before outcome.
Respondent's procedure allowed adjournment for new points.
preferred_ws_destination: Failure to comply with respondent's own disciplinary procedure
Failure to address contradictions or new points raised by claimant
ws_theme_dictionary.duplication_guardrail: T16 is during-hearing procedural safeguard failure. Do not merge it with T11 pre-hearing prejudice or T18 appeal failure.
forbidden_uses: Do not speculate what further investigation would have found.
Do not introduce unnamed witnesses beyond the WS.
Do not turn the theme into generic legal submissions.
ws_theme_dictionary.example_mapping_language: Signal: 'The employer should have adjourned to investigate new points raised at the disciplinary hearing.' → Map to T16.

ws_theme_dictionary.theme_name: Comparator Evidence and Differential Treatment
ws_theme_dictionary.definition: The claimant's comparator case: another employee with materially similar role, reporting line, and work allocation allegedly failed to meet the performance expectations applied to the claimant over a comparable period, without disciplinary action, while the claimant's equivalent or lesser shortfall was treated as evidence of misconduct. This theme is limited to identifiable comparator or differential-treatment evidence, not general team-average metrics.
include_when: A signal concerns comparator evidence.
A signal concerns differential treatment of another employee or peer.
A signal concerns a materially similar employee, role, reporting line, allegation, work allocation, or performance standard being treated differently.
A signal concerns appeal failure to investigate comparator evidence.
A signal concerns whether standards were applied consistently between employees.
exclude_when: The signal concerns the claimant's own team-average ticket comparison — map to T10.
The signal concerns appeal failure generally — map to T18 unless comparator-specific.
The signal concerns role standards generally — map to T01, T03, or T19.
common_subthemes: Comparable role, reporting line, work allocation, or standard.
Material similarity between claimant and comparator must be evidenced.
Comparator allegedly had equivalent or more serious shortfall.
Comparator was not disciplined or was treated materially more favourably.
Appeal outcome did not engage with comparator.
preferred_ws_destination: Comparator evidence raised at appeal
Appeal outcome's failure to address comparator
evidence_anchor_types: Comparator performance, scheduling, or work-allocation data
DSAR or internal records supporting comparator facts
ws_theme_dictionary.duplication_guardrail: T17 has priority over T18 for comparator-specific signals. T10 is not comparator evidence; it covers claimant's own objective output and team-average data.
forbidden_uses: Do not identify comparator by name unless already in WS or permitted.
Do not assert identical circumstances unless evidenced.
Do not imply comparator misconduct beyond the factual comparison.
ws_theme_dictionary.example_mapping_language: Signal: 'Another employee in a similar role was treated differently.' → Map to T17.

ws_theme_dictionary.theme_name: Appeal Failure to Cure Original Defects
ws_theme_dictionary.definition: The claimant's case that the appeal did not provide a genuine independent cure of material original defects because it failed to engage with specific appeal grounds, central factual or logical points, new evidence, or objective counter-evidence, and instead confirmed or rationalised the original decision. This theme requires an appeal-stage defect, not merely repetition of every first-instance flaw.
include_when: A signal concerns adequacy or independence of the appeal process and identifies a specific appeal-stage failure.
A signal concerns whether the appeal cured original defects after the claimant raised them in appeal grounds.
A signal concerns appeal reliance on unsupported late oral recall or new adverse material without proper testing.
A signal concerns appeal failure to reconcile the original allegation with objective evidence or new evidence raised on appeal.
A signal concerns appeal delay, ratification, or confirmation rather than genuine reconsideration.
exclude_when: The signal is comparator-specific — map to T17.
The signal is predetermination-specific — map to T15.
The signal concerns disciplinary-stage failure to adjourn — map to T16.
The signal concerns hearing minutes specifically — map to T13.
The signal concerns the original investigation or decision only, without a distinct appeal-stage defect — map to the relevant first-instance theme.
common_subthemes: Appeal did not engage with logical inconsistency of avoidance-by-working.
Appeal preferred late oral recall over contemporaneous documents.
Appeal did not reconcile outcome with ticket figures and badge data.
Appeal did not cure disclosure or evidential defects.
Appeal confirmed original decision rather than reconsidering it.
preferred_ws_destination: Appeal outcome analysis
Appeal hearing account
ws_theme_dictionary.duplication_guardrail: T18 requires a distinct appeal-stage failure to cure, reconsider, test, or engage with specific grounds. If the signal is comparator-specific, use T17. If specifically about appeal-period predetermination or closed mind, use T15. Do not map ordinary first-instance investigation defects to T18 unless the appeal failed to cure them after they were raised.
forbidden_uses: Do not speculate about internal appeal motives.
Do not say the appeal ignored an issue unless the outcome letter supports that.
Do not merge predetermination evidence into T18 where T15 is more specific.
ws_theme_dictionary.example_mapping_language: Signal: 'The appeal did not engage with specific appeal grounds and simply confirmed the original decision despite the defects being raised.' → Map to T18 unless T15 or T17 is more specific.

ws_theme_dictionary.theme_name: Retrospective or Mismatched Standard Applied to Earlier Conduct
ws_theme_dictionary.definition: The claimant's case that conduct, performance, or data from one period, role, policy, or expectation framework was later judged by a different or later standard that did not govern the conduct at the time. This includes senior-role conduct later judged by Trade Desk Analyst or queue-centric expectations, but also covers comparable retrospective application of later rules, changed duties, or newly articulated standards.
include_when: A signal concerns which role, policy, rule, or standard applied to the alleged conduct at the time.
A signal concerns conduct later judged by expectations from a different role, later policy, changed duty, or newly articulated standard.
A signal concerns senior-role conduct later judged by junior or queue-centric expectations.

A signal concerns a specific role change, policy change, or expectation change that preceded or coincided with disciplinary escalation, where the underlying conduct or system behaviour had not itself changed.
A signal concerns escalation occurring only after a role, policy, or expectation change despite unchanged conduct or system behaviour.
exclude_when: The signal concerns role evolution history only — map to T01.
The signal concerns management project-priority instructions — map to T02.
The signal concerns whether a duty was continuous or conditional in practice at the time — map to T03.
common_subthemes: Earlier period governed by a different role, rule, or expectation framework.
Later standard applied to earlier conduct.
Senior role: project-led and escalation-focused.
Trade Desk Analyst role: queue-centric.
System behaviour identified during senior role but escalated after role change.
Disciplinary data drawn from senior-role period.
No change in conduct or risk profile; only the standard or role label changed.
preferred_ws_destination: Escalation only after role or standard change
Mismatch between conduct period and standard applied
evidence_anchor_types: ET3 statement on Trade Desk Analyst role
Absence of escalation during senior role
ws_theme_dictionary.duplication_guardrail: T19 has priority where the issue is mismatch between the standard applied and the period, role, policy, or expectation framework governing the conduct. Use T01 for role history only; T02 for positive instructions; and T03 for whether the duty was conditional at the time.
forbidden_uses: Do not suggest the role change itself was unlawful unless supported.
Do not introduce constructive dismissal arguments.
Do not make contractual claims beyond the WS.
ws_theme_dictionary.example_mapping_language: Signal: 'The employer judged earlier conduct by a later or different standard that did not govern the claimant's work at the time.' → Map to T19.

ws_theme_dictionary.theme_name: Speculative, Low-Value, or Potentially Distracting Material — Risk Control
ws_theme_dictionary.definition: A quarantine and review theme for material that may be speculative, weakly evidenced, peripheral, duplicative, inferential beyond the WS, or distracting from stronger documentary themes. This includes Fishbowl material, broad motive speculation, unsupported assertions about what missing logs would show, and any new argument not traceable to the WS.
include_when: A signal requires factual inference not supported by the WS.
A signal relies on Fishbowl or anonymous forum material.
A signal speculates about what missing logs would have proved.
A signal attributes improper motive without documentary support.
A signal duplicates an existing stronger theme using different language.
A proposed addition is not traceable to WS_TEXT.
exclude_when: The signal fits any of T01–T19 with documentary support.
The signal is weak but still traceable to a specific substantive theme — map to that theme with REVIEW_MANUALLY.
The signal concerns predetermination evidence from documents, scripts, internal communications, or appeal-period communications — map to T15, not T20.
common_subthemes: Fishbowl content: background only, not proof of specific facts.
Speculation about management motive.
Speculation about missing logs.
Unanchored reputational-damage assertions.
Discrimination or minority-background implications requiring careful evidential review.
Duplicative stylistic variants of existing themes.
preferred_ws_destination: Review only — no automatic WS destination
evidence_anchor_types: None — T20 is a control gate, not an evidential destination
ws_theme_dictionary.duplication_guardrail: T20 is not a substantive theme. It is a quarantine cage. Use it only when no specific theme fits or where the point is speculative, weak, duplicative, or potentially distracting. Fishbowl must be mapped here only, not to T15.
forbidden_uses: Do not use T20 to suppress evidenced arguments.
Do not map difficult but supported points here merely because they are complex.
Do not treat T20 as a drafting destination.
ws_theme_dictionary.example_mapping_language: Signal: 'Fishbowl posts suggest a wider dismissal culture.' → Map to T20 and flag REVIEW_MANUALLY or REMOVE.

global_mapping_rules.default_rule: Every judgment signal must be mapped to exactly one primary theme from T01 to T20. Use the most specific applicable theme, then resolve conflicts using theme_priority. If no theme fits, map to T20 only if speculative, weak, duplicative, or unsupported; otherwise flag REVIEW_MANUALLY.
global_mapping_rules.new_theme_rule: No new themes may be created in this dictionary version. If a signal appears to require a new theme, map to the nearest existing theme with REVIEW_MANUALLY, or T20 if speculative or unsupported.
global_mapping_rules.duplication_rule: Each signal must be mapped to exactly one primary theme. Secondary candidate themes may be recorded in downstream mapping outputs as cross_reference_theme_ids, but this field is not pre-populated in the dictionary and must not create duplicate action items.
global_mapping_rules.evidence_rule: Each mapped signal must be traceable to WS_TEXT and at least one evidence_anchor_type. If the evidence anchor is missing or incomplete, use ADD EVIDENCE ANCHOR rather than ADD unless a genuinely missing factual section is identified.
global_mapping_rules.speculation_rule: Any signal requiring facts not present or clearly latent in WS_TEXT must be mapped to T20 and flagged REVIEW_MANUALLY or DO_NOT_USE. Do not let external case facts create new WS allegations.
global_mapping_rules.chronology_rule: When a signal concerns timing or consistency of the claimant's explanation, T06 takes priority over all other themes. When a signal concerns earlier conduct judged by a later or different role, policy, or expectation standard, T19 takes priority over T01, T02, and T03.
global_mapping_rules.risk_rule: Canonical priority order: (1) T06_CONSISTENT_DEFENCE for timing, consistency, changed-account, and rebuttal-mapping signals; (2) T19_RETROSPECTIVE_STANDARD for earlier conduct judged by a later or different role, policy, or expectation standard; (3) T01_ROLE_EVOLUTION for structural role history; (4) T02_MANAGEMENT_DIRECTION for positive management instruction; (5) T03_QUEUE_SITUATIONAL for whether an alleged duty was continuous or conditional in practice; (6) T05_TECHNICAL_CAUSATION for technical causation versus disciplinary inference; (7) T08_NO_QUEUE_TIME_DISTINCTION for event-data alignment with relevant duty periods; (8) T09_MISSING_LOGS for missing raw system evidence; (9) T10_OBJECTIVE_COUNTER_EVIDENCE for objective output, attendance, access, performance, and feedback evidence; (10) T04_JULY2023_NO_INSTRUCTION for management awareness or acquiescence followed by continued work without correction; (11) T07_NO_PRIOR_WARNING for the broader no-warning pattern; (12) T11_SHORT_NOTICE_PROCEDURAL_PREJUDICE for pre-hearing notice and preparation prejudice; (13) T16_FAILURE_TO_ADJOURN for during-hearing failure to adjourn or investigate new points; (14) T12_HOSTILE_HEARING_LINGUISTIC for hearing conduct and communication disadvantage; (15) T13_INACCURATE_MINUTES for inaccurate or selective minutes; (16) T17_COMPARATOR_TREATMENT for comparator-specific signals; (17) T15_PREDETERMINATION for closed-mind, pre-decision, scripted-outcome, or appeal-period disclosure predetermination signals; (18) T18_APPEAL_NO_CURE for distinct appeal-stage failure to cure specific defects where T15 or T17 is not more specific; (19) T14_FAILURE_TO_VERIFY_EVIDENCE for residual overall evidential assessment only where no more specific evidence theme applies; (20) T20_RISK_CONTROL for speculative, low-value, duplicative, or unsupported material.
global_mapping_rules.theme_id_rule: The numeric part of theme_id is not a priority rank. Do not infer priority from T01, T02, etc.
global_mapping_rules.theme_priority_rule: theme_priority is the canonical conflict-resolution order. Values must be unique integers from 1 to 20 and must exactly match risk_rule.
global_mapping_rules.action_rule: REFRAME remains a valid permitted action where a judgment signal is adversarial or dangerously worded but can be safely mapped into an existing WS theme. REFRAME must not create new facts.

amendments_from_v1: Added theme_priority to every theme for machine conflict resolution.
Defined T03 boundaries so conditional-duty analysis does not repeat management-direction content.
Narrowed T14 so it cannot swallow T08, T09, T10, T11, or T16.
Removed Fishbowl from T15 and confined it to T20 risk control.
Softened T12 definition to frame impairment as the claimant's account rather than dictionary-level conclusion.
Reduced ADD permissions for already-present substantive themes and reserved ADD mainly for missing facts, evidence anchors, or latent/fallback structures.
Resolved priority-rule ambiguity between T17 comparator signals and T18 appeal-no-cure signals.
Clarified that T20 is a quarantine/control gate, not a substantive drafting destination.
Broadened T04 from a July-only event into management awareness/acquiescence followed by continued work without correction.
Broadened T03 from queue-only conditionality into conditional or situational work-obligation analysis.
Broadened T08 from crash/queue segmentation into event-data alignment with relevant duty periods.
Broadened T19 from junior-role retrospective standards into any later or different standard applied to earlier conduct.
Broadened T15 from the exact script/WhatsApp strands into documentary or communication evidence of closed mind or pre-decision.
Tightened T14 as a residual overall-evidence theme only after more specific themes are excluded.
Tightened T18 so it requires a distinct appeal-stage failure rather than repeating first-instance defects.
notes: T01 and T19 remain intentionally distinct: T01 is role history; T19 is retrospective application of a later or different standard.
T04 and T07 remain intentionally distinct: T04 requires management awareness or acquiescence followed by continued work without correction; T07 is the broader no-warning pattern.
T08 and T09 remain intentionally distinct: T08 is event-data methodology; T09 is missing raw evidence.
T10 remains the consolidation bucket for objective counter-evidence to avoid fragmentation across ticket figures, badge records, and performance records.
T15 now excludes Fishbowl entirely. Fishbowl belongs only in T20 unless independently evidenced by another WS anchor.
T17 has priority over T18 for comparator-specific signals, even where the comparator issue arose at appeal.
T18 captures distinct appeal-stage failure but must not absorb predetermination-specific, comparator-specific, or purely first-instance investigation signals.
T20 should be used sparingly and defensively, not as a default for hard classification problems.
validation_rules: theme_priority values must be unique integers from 1 to 20.
theme_priority values must exactly match the canonical order in global_mapping_rules.risk_rule.
theme_id values must remain stable and must not be renumbered to match priority.
cross_reference_theme_ids may appear only in downstream mapping outputs, not as required dictionary fields.
REFRAME remains a valid action only where it maps an unsafe/adversarial framing into an existing theme without creating new facts.
amendments_from_v1_1: Harmonised all theme_priority values with the canonical risk_rule order.
Resolved the T12/T16 priority misalignment: T16 is priority 13 and T12 is priority 14.
Resolved lower-cluster offset: T17 is 16, T15 is 17, T18 is 18, T14 is 19, T20 is 20.
Clarified that theme_id is a stable identifier and not a priority rank.
Clarified that cross_reference_theme_ids is a downstream mapping-output field, not a dictionary-entry field.
Restored REFRAME as a permitted action for T05, T06, T12, and T20.
Revised T06 example_mapping_language so adversarial inconsistency signals are used for rebuttal mapping, not positive WS insertion.
amendments_from_v1_2: Generalised T05 definition and include_when to remove system-specific names; principle now reads as technical occurrence vs culpable intent inference.
Generalised T10 definition to remove case-specific figures; objective counter-evidence pattern now described without exact ticket counts, hours, or system names.
Generalised T17 definition to remove case-specific comparator facts.
Generalised T02 include_when items 3 and 4 to remove evaluation-date and DSAR-specific hooks.
Generalised T12 definition and include_when to remove the case-specific 'we' comment; dismissive conduct now described in general terms.
Generalised T01 include_when item 3 to remove specific job title.
Generalised T07 definition to remove case-specific conduct labels.
Generalised T09 definition to remove case-specific system terminology.
Generalised T19 include_when item 4 to remove specific role name and ET3 reference.
amendments_from_v1_3: Generalised T01 title, definition, common_subthemes, evidence anchors, and example away from claimant-specific dates, title, and queue metrics.
Generalised T02 title, definition, include_when, common_subthemes, destinations, evidence anchors, and example away from project-specific wording.
Generalised T06 definition, include_when, common_subthemes, evidence anchors, and guardrail away from specific meeting dates and technical facts while preserving priority-1 chronology logic.
Cleaned T10 common_subthemes, destinations, evidence anchors, and guardrail to remove exact figures and product-specific references.
Generalised T12 title, definition, include_when, subthemes, destination, and forbidden uses away from case-specific comments and named individuals.
Generalised T16 definition, include_when, subthemes, destination, and witness wording away from queue/crash and named-individual anchors.
Generalised T17 include_when, common_subthemes, and evidence anchors away from same-manager, same-Team-Leader, queue-hour, and ticket-count anchors.
